steps.title: See your user
steps.description: Run whoami to see your user.

steps.title: Print working directory
steps.description: Try pwd and ls -la in the terminal.

steps.title: Create a practice directory
steps.description: Run: mkdir practice && cd practice (from your home or /).

hintLevels.title: Concept
lines: Learn who you are on the system, where you are in the filesystem, and how to list files.

hintLevels.title: Full example
lines: mkdir -p demo && cd demo && touch hello.txt && ls -la

steps.title: Initialize a repository
steps.description: Run git init in a project directory.

steps.title: Stage a file
steps.description: Create a file and run git add on it.

hintLevels.title: Concept
lines: Create a new directory, turn it into a Git repo, track a file, and record your first commit.

steps.title: Pull the nginx image
steps.description: Download the official nginx image from Docker Hub.
steps.why: You need the image locally before you can start a container from it — same as installing an app before opening it.
steps.failureHint: Pull the image first, wait for it to finish, then click Check.

failurePatterns.title: Docker isn’t reachable
failurePatterns.hint: Start Docker Desktop (or the Docker service), wait until it’s healthy, then try again.

validation.command: bash -lc "docker images --format '{{.Repository}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -qx nginx && echo STEP_OK || echo STEP_FAIL"

steps.title: Run nginx in the background
steps.description: Publish container port 80 to host port 8080 so you can reach the app.
steps.why: `-d` runs it detached; `-p 8080:80` maps ports so your browser (or curl) can hit nginx.
steps.failureHint: Run the container with port mapping, wait a second for it to start, then click Check.

validation.command: bash -lc "docker ps --format '{{.Image}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^nginx(:|$|@)' >/dev/null && echo STEP_OK || echo STEP_FAIL"

steps.title: List running containers
steps.description: Use Docker’s listing command to see what’s running.
steps.why: `docker ps` is how you verify containers, names, ports, and uptime — your day-to-day status command.
steps.failureHint: Run docker ps in the terminal and confirm you see the nginx container, then click Check.

validation.command: bash -lc "docker ps 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -q CONTAINER && echo STEP_OK || echo STEP_FAIL"

steps.title: Quick recall
steps.description: Lock in what you just practiced.
steps.why: Remembering the right command helps you debug real environments without hunting docs.
steps.question: What command lists running containers?
steps.failureHint: Hint: it’s the same command you used in the previous step.

validation.command: bash -lc "docker ps --format '{{.Names}} {{.Image}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -q nginx && echo STEP_OK || echo STEP_FAIL"

hintLevels.title: Concept
lines: Pull a public image from Docker Hub, run it in the background with a port mapping, then list running containers.

hintLevels.title: Full example
lines: docker pull nginx && docker run -d --name web -p 8080:80 nginx && docker ps

steps.title: Create main.tf
steps.description: Add a minimal Terraform configuration file.

steps.title: Run terraform init
steps.description: Initialize providers in your working directory.

validation.command: bash -lc 'terraform state list 2>/dev/null | grep -q . && echo STATE_OK || echo STATE_MISSING'

hintLevels.title: Concept
lines: Create a working directory, add a minimal Terraform config, initialize providers, then apply the plan.

hintLevels.title: Full example
lines: # Add main.tf with a null_resource or tiny provider block, then: terraform init && terraform apply -auto-approve

steps.title: Confirm nginx is listening
steps.description: Use Open App or curl; nginx listens on port 80 in the container.

steps.title: Inspect web root
steps.description: List files under /usr/share/nginx/html in the terminal.

hintLevels.title: Concept
lines: Nginx listens on port 80 in the container; the platform maps it to a temporary URL under /lab/<sessionId>.
